grammar.description: Grounds the affirmation in a completed past action or state — celebrating past strength and achievement.
grammar.name: ✅ Finite Indicative Active Past Simple First Singular
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express the action plainly without specifying duration or extent over time.
- Use the past tense.
- Subject is 'I' (first-person singular). IMPORTANT: In English, the first-person singular present tense uses the bare stem — 'I accept', 'I trust', 'I allow', 'I guard', 'I choose', 'I connect', 'I possess' are all correctly conjugated finite verbs in present simple. Do NOT reject them for being 'base form' or 'unconjugated' — they ARE correctly conjugated.

affirmations.text: I was the center of my own light
I found my balance in the middle
I achieved harmony within my being

grammar.description: Asserts a present-moment truth directly and simply — the most fundamental form of affirmation.
grammar.name: ⭐ Finite Indicative Active Present Simple First Singular
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express the action plainly without specifying duration or extent over time.
- Use the present tense.
- Subject is 'I' (first-person singular). IMPORTANT: In English, the first-person singular present tense uses the bare stem — 'I accept', 'I trust', 'I allow', 'I guard', 'I choose', 'I connect', 'I possess' are all correctly conjugated finite verbs in present simple. Do NOT reject them for being 'base form' or 'unconjugated' — they ARE correctly conjugated.

affirmations.text: I possess inner balance and perfect harmony
I center my life with clear and steady awareness
I radiate stable light through my true self

grammar.description: Projects confident intention toward the future — affirming what one will be or achieve.
grammar.name: ⏭️ Finite Indicative Active Future Simple First Singular
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express the action plainly without specifying duration or extent over time.
- Use the future tense with 'will'. Keep the structure simple: 'I will [verb] [direct object/complement]'. Do NOT write complex object clauses like 'I will see my thoughts are...' — use 'I will recognize my thoughts' or 'I will see clearly' instead. Do NOT end the affirmation with 'now' — that word makes the sentence feel present tense, not future.
- Subject is 'I' (first-person singular). IMPORTANT: In English, the first-person singular present tense uses the bare stem — 'I accept', 'I trust', 'I allow', 'I guard', 'I choose', 'I connect', 'I possess' are all correctly conjugated finite verbs in present simple. Do NOT reject them for being 'base form' or 'unconjugated' — they ARE correctly conjugated.

affirmations.text: I will find my inner center of balance
I will establish harmony in all my life
I will radiate my true light outward

grammar.description: Affirms continuous progress that began in the past and is still unfolding — capturing momentum and sustained effort.
grammar.name: 🔄 Finite Indicative Active Present Perfect Progressive First Singular
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Use the perfect progressive: 'I have been [verb]ing...'. The sentence must open with 'I have been'. Coordinated verbs must also use the -ing form, e.g., 'I have been growing and becoming...'. Do NOT end the affirmation with 'now' — the perfect progressive already implies ongoing action up to the present.
- Use the present tense.
- Subject is 'I' (first-person singular). IMPORTANT: In English, the first-person singular present tense uses the bare stem — 'I accept', 'I trust', 'I allow', 'I guard', 'I choose', 'I connect', 'I possess' are all correctly conjugated finite verbs in present simple. Do NOT reject them for being 'base form' or 'unconjugated' — they ARE correctly conjugated.

affirmations.text: I have been finding my center in all things
I have been balancing my life with ease
I have been radiating my true light always

grammar.description: Visualizes a future moment where something is already accomplished — affirming from the vantage point of completion.
grammar.name: 🎯 Finite Indicative Active Future Perfect Perfective First Singular
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express the action as a complete, bounded whole — viewed start-to-finish.
- Use FUTURE PERFECT tense: 'I will have [past participle]...'. This is DIFFERENT from present perfect ('I have [past participle]'). 'I will have become...', 'I will have achieved...', 'I will have created...' are all valid future perfect. The 'will have' structure is mandatory. Do NOT end the affirmation with 'now' — that word undercuts the future-looking perspective.
- Subject is 'I' (first-person singular). IMPORTANT: In English, the first-person singular present tense uses the bare stem — 'I accept', 'I trust', 'I allow', 'I guard', 'I choose', 'I connect', 'I possess' are all correctly conjugated finite verbs in present simple. Do NOT reject them for being 'base form' or 'unconjugated' — they ARE correctly conjugated.

affirmations.text: I will have found my true center of balance
I will have established perfect harmony within me
I will have integrated all my parts successfully

grammar.description: Affirms shared strength and collective identity — embracing connection and belonging with others.
grammar.name: 🤝 Finite Indicative Active Present Simple First Plural
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express the action plainly without specifying duration or extent over time.
- Use the present tense.
- Subject is 'we' (first person plural).

affirmations.text: We find our center in perfect balance together
We harmonize our parts in peaceful alignment
We radiate warmth and clarity as one

grammar.description: Speaks a present truth about another person — broadening perspective beyond the self.
grammar.name: 👤 Finite Indicative Active Present Simple Third
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express the action plainly without specifying duration or extent over time.
- Use the present tense.
- CRITICAL: Do NOT use 'I'. The subject must be third-person: 'he', 'she', 'it', 'they', or a third-person noun phrase (e.g., 'the light', 'the roots'). Never start the sentence with 'I'. IMPORTANT: Singular subjects (he/she/it/a noun) take a verb with -s (e.g., 'flows', 'balances', 'brings'). Plural subjects (they/the roots/the branches) correctly take a verb WITHOUT -s (e.g., 'flow', 'grow', 'establish').

affirmations.text: The center balances all inner elements perfectly
Harmony establishes stable relationships in existence
Light radiates true equilibrium through alignment

grammar.description: Affirms capability and possibility — what the speaker can do or is able to accomplish.
grammar.name: 💪 Finite Potential Active Present Simple First Singular
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- CRITICAL: The main predicate MUST begin with 'can', 'could', or 'am/is/are able to'. Valid structures: 'I can [verb]', 'I could [verb]', 'I am able to [verb]'. The verb that follows the auxiliary must be a simple action verb or adjective — keep it direct and simple. Do NOT use complex object clauses like 'I can see that...' or 'I can feel that...' — instead write 'I can see clearly', 'I can feel peace'. Do NOT use a bare indicative verb as the main predicate without a preceding potential auxiliary (e.g., do NOT write 'I trust...' or 'I believe...' as a standalone clause without 'can'/'could'/'able to').
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express the action plainly without specifying duration or extent over time.
- Use the present tense.
- Subject is 'I' (first-person singular). IMPORTANT: In English, the first-person singular present tense uses the bare stem — 'I accept', 'I trust', 'I allow', 'I guard', 'I choose', 'I connect', 'I possess' are all correctly conjugated finite verbs in present simple. Do NOT reject them for being 'base form' or 'unconjugated' — they ARE correctly conjugated.

affirmations.text: I can find my center and achieve true balance
I am able to integrate all parts within me
I can radiate harmony from my inner self

grammar.description: Expresses a sincere wish or deep longing — voicing the heart's desire with openness and hope.
grammar.name: 🙏 Finite Optative Active First Singular
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- Express a deep wish, longing, or hope — NOT a request for permission. 'May I [verb]' here means 'I wish that I [verb]' (optative wish), NOT 'Am I allowed to [verb]'. 'If only I could [verb]', 'Would that I might [verb]', and 'May I [verb]' are all valid optative forms expressing yearning. The tone should be longing, aspiration, or heartfelt desire — not a yes/no question about permission.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Subject is 'I' (first-person singular). IMPORTANT: In English, the first-person singular present tense uses the bare stem — 'I accept', 'I trust', 'I allow', 'I guard', 'I choose', 'I connect', 'I possess' are all correctly conjugated finite verbs in present simple. Do NOT reject them for being 'base form' or 'unconjugated' — they ARE correctly conjugated.

affirmations.text: May I find my true center and perfect balance
If only I could harmonize all my inner parts
Would that I might achieve settled, radiant fulfillment

grammar.description: Commands the self or another to act — activating, direct, and rousing.
grammar.name: ❗ Finite Imperative Active Second Singular
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- Use the imperative mood: start the sentence with a bare infinitive verb — NO explicit subject. The implicit (unstated) subject is always 'you'. Subordinate clauses in the same sentence may use any voice, tense, or person — only the MAIN verb (the one that starts the sentence) must be bare imperative. Do NOT write 'You should...', 'You must...', or any sentence that starts with 'I'.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Subject/address is 'you' (second person).

affirmations.text: Be centered and find your inner balance
Align your life and achieve true harmony
Know your place and embrace your light

grammar.description: Challenges limiting beliefs through a rhetorical question — confronting self-doubt head-on.
grammar.name: ❓ Finite Interrogative Active Present First Singular Negative
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- CRITICAL: The sentence MUST be a QUESTION and MUST end with '?'. Required patterns: 'Am I not...?', 'Have I not...?', 'Can I not...?', 'Do I not...?'. The '?' at the very end of the sentence is MANDATORY — never omit it. Do NOT write a declarative statement. 'Can' and 'have' are valid finite auxiliaries.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Include negation using 'not', 'never', 'no longer', or 'without'. Compound predicates that combine a negation with a positive clause are VALID (e.g., 'I am not stagnant but flow with abundance', 'I am not fearful but trust fully'). The presence of a positive second clause does NOT make the affirmation fail the NEGATIVE requirement as long as the negation word is present.
- Use the present tense.
- Subject is 'I' (first-person singular). IMPORTANT: In English, the first-person singular present tense uses the bare stem — 'I accept', 'I trust', 'I allow', 'I guard', 'I choose', 'I connect', 'I possess' are all correctly conjugated finite verbs in present simple. Do NOT reject them for being 'base form' or 'unconjugated' — they ARE correctly conjugated.

affirmations.text: Am I not centered in this perfect harmony
Have I not found my true stable midpoint
Can I not radiate my inner brilliance now

grammar.description: Names a purpose or aspiration in its purest infinitive form — to be, to do, to become.
grammar.name: ♾️ Infinitive Active Simple
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Start with the base infinitive: 'To [verb]...' — no subject or conjugation. Objects, complements, and prepositional phrases after 'To [verb]' are all allowed — 'To acknowledge support without doubt' and 'To create a nurturing space with care' are both VALID. The only requirement is that the sentence begins with 'To [infinitive verb]'.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express the action plainly without specifying duration or extent over time.

affirmations.text: To find center with peace
To embrace balance without conflict
To align with your true light

grammar.description: Affirms through process and action — celebrating the doing and becoming, not merely the state of being.
grammar.name: 💡 Gerund Active
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Begin with a gerund: an -ing verb form that acts as the grammatical SUBJECT of the main clause. Required sentence structure: '[Gerund phrase] [finite main verb] [complement]'. The gerund phrase IS the noun-subject; the sentence MUST have a separate finite verb after it. VALID examples: 'Being present opens doors', 'Trusting yourself builds strength', 'Navigating challenges requires courage', 'Releasing fear allows peace', 'Recognizing truth frees the mind'. The finite verb is the word directly AFTER the gerund phrase (e.g., 'opens', 'builds', 'requires', 'allows', 'frees'). Do NOT write a participial phrase where the -ing word modifies a separate subject (e.g., do NOT write 'Stepping forward, I trust...' or 'Trusting the path, I grow...'). Do NOT write an incomplete phrase without a finite main verb. CRITICAL: ANY affirmation matching '[Gerund -ing phrase] [finite main verb] [complement or object]' is VALID regardless of vocabulary — e.g., 'Giving support creates abundant connection', 'Receiving kindness fosters inner balance', 'Observing growth reveals true value'. Do NOT reject because the vocabulary differs from the provided examples — structure is all that matters.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.

affirmations.text: Centering awareness brings perfect internal harmony
Integrating light establishes balanced personal structure
Finding the midpoint creates true life equilibrium

grammar.description: Affirms being received, held, or supported — openness to grace, love, and forces beyond the self.
grammar.name: 🌊 Finite Indicative Passive Present Simple First Singular
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- CRITICAL: The MAIN clause must use PASSIVE voice — the subject receives the action. Pattern: 'I am/was/have been [past participle]'. Use naturally passive past participles such as: guided, held, supported, loved, carried, blessed, seen, known, called, drawn, moved, shaped, filled, grounded, anchored, surrounded, embraced, lifted, held. A single optional 'by [agent]' phrase is allowed. CRITICAL: do NOT use two separate 'by ...' phrases in one sentence — e.g., NEVER write 'I am guided by X by Y' or 'I am held by X by Y'. If you want to express two agents, combine them: 'I am guided by wisdom and courage'. Subordinate clauses and infinitive phrases (e.g., 'ready to feel', 'able to see') may use active voice. Do NOT write active main clauses like 'I trust', 'I choose', or 'I feel'.
- Express the action plainly without specifying duration or extent over time.
- Use the present tense.
- Subject is 'I' (first-person singular). IMPORTANT: In English, the first-person singular present tense uses the bare stem — 'I accept', 'I trust', 'I allow', 'I guard', 'I choose', 'I connect', 'I possess' are all correctly conjugated finite verbs in present simple. Do NOT reject them for being 'base form' or 'unconjugated' — they ARE correctly conjugated.

affirmations.text: I am centered by the sun's perfect balance
I am held by inner harmony and light
I am grounded by my own true midpoint

grammar.description: Reinforces a repeated practice or pattern — affirming what the speaker consistently does or believes.
grammar.name: 🔁 Finite Indicative Active Present Habitual First Singular
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express a recurring action using 'always', 'regularly', 'consistently', or 'every day'.
- Use the present tense.
- Subject is 'I' (first-person singular). IMPORTANT: In English, the first-person singular present tense uses the bare stem — 'I accept', 'I trust', 'I allow', 'I guard', 'I choose', 'I connect', 'I possess' are all correctly conjugated finite verbs in present simple. Do NOT reject them for being 'base form' or 'unconjugated' — they ARE correctly conjugated.

affirmations.text: I always find my inner center and balance
I regularly establish harmony in my life
I consistently radiate stable, joyful awareness

grammar.description: Affirms by negating a false belief or limitation — releasing what is not true about the self.
grammar.name: 🫷 Finite Indicative Active Present Simple First Singular Negative
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Include negation using 'not', 'never', 'no longer', or 'without'. Compound predicates that combine a negation with a positive clause are VALID (e.g., 'I am not stagnant but flow with abundance', 'I am not fearful but trust fully'). The presence of a positive second clause does NOT make the affirmation fail the NEGATIVE requirement as long as the negation word is present.
- Express the action plainly without specifying duration or extent over time.
- Use the present tense.
- Subject is 'I' (first-person singular). IMPORTANT: In English, the first-person singular present tense uses the bare stem — 'I accept', 'I trust', 'I allow', 'I guard', 'I choose', 'I connect', 'I possess' are all correctly conjugated finite verbs in present simple. Do NOT reject them for being 'base form' or 'unconjugated' — they ARE correctly conjugated.

affirmations.text: I am not scattered but centered in my light
I do not seek external validation for my worth
I am not divided but embrace inner harmony
